betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Ethanol§f

§aBiofuel from sugar — delivers 275 kTU and 687 kHE per bucket!§f

§bEthanol§f is a flammable, combustible, polluting liquid (NFPA 2/3/0) with high fuel grade. It provides §e275 kTU§f and §e687 kHE§f per bucket.

§6Production§f:
§b• Chemical Plant§f (2.5s) — 10× §bSugar§f → 1,000 mB §eEthanol§f
§b• Industrial Liquefaction§f — §bSugar§f → 100 mB, §bMustard Willow§f → 50 mB, §bHemp§f → 150 mB
§b• Combination Oven§f — §bSugar Canes§f → 2 Sugar + 50 mB Ethanol

§6Key Uses§f:
§b• §eBiofuel§f — Chemical Plant: 1,500 mB Biogas + 250 mB Ethanol → 1,000 mB Biofuel (3s)
§b• §eEngine Lubricant§f — Industrial Mixer: 200 mB Ethanol + 800 mB Fish Oil → 1,000 mB Lubricant
§b• §eCombustion Power§f — directly burn for HE or TU in appropriate generators

§eTip§f: Sugar is renewable through sugar cane farming. Automate with a farms and pipe the sugar directly to a Chemical Plant for passive ethanol production. One stack of sugar yields ~6 buckets of ethanol!
